name: Fiber (Go)
what_it_does: Express-inspired Go web framework built on top of Fasthttp (not net/http). Fiber is designed for developers coming from Node.js/Express who want familiar API patterns with Go's performance. Claims to be the fastest Go web framework by using Fasthttp instead of standard library HTTP. Great for agent developers transitioning from Node.js to Go.
use_cases: Build high-performance Go agent APIs with Express-like routing familiar to Node.js developers
Create agent microservices in Go with Fiber's extensive built-in middleware (rate limit, cache, compress)
Implement agent API servers leveraging Fasthttp's zero-allocation response writing for minimal GC pressure
Build WebSocket agent servers with Fiber's WebSocket middleware built on Fasthttp
Deploy agent services where Express-to-Go migration is needed with minimal learning curve
not_for: Code that needs stdlib net/http compatibility — Fiber uses Fasthttp which is NOT compatible with net/http handlers
Projects requiring net/http ecosystem middleware — most Go middleware assumes net/http, incompatible with Fiber
Teams not familiar with Fasthttp's limitations (no HTTP/2, different context model)
best_when: You're a Node.js/Express developer moving to Go and want familiar API patterns with extremely high throughput.
avoid_when: You need net/http compatibility, HTTP/2, or want to use standard Go middleware ecosystem — use Gin or Echo instead.

auth.notes: No built-in auth — implement via middleware. Fiber middleware ecosystem includes JWT and KeyAuth.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — runs on your infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error handling via c.Next(err) pattern. Custom error handler via app.ErrorHandler. Fasthttp-level errors surface with HTTP status and message.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP handler idempotency is application responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: Fiber is NOT compatible with net/http — c.Context() returns *fasthttp.RequestCtx, not http.Request; existing Go middleware won't work
HTTP/2 is not supported by Fasthttp — if your agent clients require HTTP/2, use Gin or Echo instead
fiber.Ctx is stack-allocated and reused — storing ctx in goroutines after handler returns causes data corruption; copy needed data before spawning goroutines
Fiber's immutable mode (app.New(fiber.Config{Immutable: true})) prevents request body reuse — understand immutable vs mutable modes for your use case
Fiber v2 to v3 (coming) has planned breaking changes — verify stability of v3 before adopting in agent services
Route grouping and middleware in Fiber uses a different API than Gin — middleware must be added before routes in the group for correct ordering

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS via standard Go TLS. Fasthttp-specific request context requires care with goroutine sharing. Built-in CORS and Helmet middleware available.
